title: The Cavalry of Rome
designIntent: DSK-composed WebGPU/WebGL-first Roman strategy route: scan over a large procedural campaign map, issue one world action at a time, read supply pressure, march corridors, morale fronts, unit cohesion, command choices, scouting vectors, flank risk, reinforcement needs, attrition forecasts, turn tempo, objective pressure, logistics readiness, and diplomatic command pressure through renderer-neutral descriptors, then preserve the older battlefield and tactical maneuver surfaces without moving reusable logic into the renderer.
standingIterationRule: Every future gameplay iteration should improve visual fidelity as a secondary goal and should not regress terrain density, vegetation descriptors, lighting mood, region readability, soldier fidelity, hex readability, maneuver feedback, command readability, order readability, logistics readiness, diplomatic command pressure, or cinematic composition.

currentGameplayStep.intent: Add a political campaign layer over the map so players can read senate mandates, tribute obligations, ally loyalty, rebellion sparks, province pacification, and triumph windows without putting campaign truth into the renderer.
currentGameplayStep.implementation: cavalry-diplomatic-command-readiness-domain-kit.js emits senate-decree-mandate, tribute-obligation-ledger, ally-loyalty-banner, rebellion-spark, province-pacification-band, triumph-window-standard, and renderer-handoff descriptors from the current campaign state. cavalry-diplomatic-command-readiness-pass.js imports NexusEngine main via CDN, computes diplomatic command readiness each frame, exposes GameHost diplomatic command state, and renders a presentation-only overlay that consumes descriptors without owning campaign simulation, input, WebGL, audio, assets, or frame-loop truth.

advanceLeft.effect: Roll 1d6 and move that many eligible Rome units from the left third.

advanceCenter.effect: Roll 1d6 and move that many eligible Rome units from the center third.

advanceRight.effect: Roll 1d6 and move that many eligible Rome units from the right third.

lineBrigade.effect: Move a connected adjacent group of Rome units.

berserk.effect: Move one Rome unit up to two spaces and attack an adjacent enemy.

scout.effect: Move one Rome unit up to three spaces.

campaignFractalDomain.tree: cavalry-campaign-fractal-domain -> campaign-readability/supply-line + march-corridor -> battlefield-readability/unit-cohesion + morale-weather + maneuver-choice -> renderer-handoff/descriptors-only
campaignFractalDomain.handoff: renderer consumes descriptor buckets only: supplyLines, marchCorridors, cohesionFields, moraleFronts, maneuverChoices

battlefieldOrdersDomain.tree: cavalry-battlefield-orders-domain -> reconnaissance/scouting-vector + flank-risk -> formation-response/reinforcement + attrition -> campaign-tempo/turn-tempo + objective-pressure -> renderer-handoff/descriptors-only
battlefieldOrdersDomain.handoff: renderer consumes descriptor buckets only: scoutingVectors, flankRiskArcs, reinforcementCallouts, attritionForecastChips, turnTempoStandards, objectivePressureBanners

logisticsReadinessDomain.tree: cavalry-logistics-readiness-domain -> sustainment/depot-radius + forage-corridor -> campaign-route/road-strain + courier-order -> seasonal-siege/siege-readiness + winter-attrition -> renderer-handoff/descriptors-only
logisticsReadinessDomain.handoff: renderer consumes descriptor buckets only: supplyDepotRadii, forageCorridors, roadStrainThreads, siegeReadinessSignals, courierOrderPulses, winterAttritionWarnings

diplomaticCommandReadinessDomain.tree: cavalry-diplomatic-command-readiness-domain -> senate-pressure/decree-mandate + tribute-obligation -> ally-frontier/ally-loyalty + rebellion-spark -> campaign-legitimacy/province-pacification + triumph-window -> renderer-handoff/descriptors-only
diplomaticCommandReadinessDomain.handoff: renderer consumes descriptor buckets only: senateDecreeMandates, tributeObligationLedgers, allyLoyaltyBanners, rebellionSparks, provincePacificationBands, triumphWindowStandards

fidelityFocus.terrain: realistic low-poly terrain with domain warping, FBM/ridged noise, blended biome colors, moisture/slope/river masks, and non-repeating landforms
fidelityFocus.mapNavigation: large pannable campaign surface with keyboard/drag pan and wheel zoom
fidelityFocus.campaignReadability: descriptor-driven supply lines, march corridors, unit cohesion fields, morale-weather fronts, and command choice bands over the campaign map
fidelityFocus.orderReadability: descriptor-driven scout vectors, flank arcs, reinforcement bars, attrition chips, order tempo standards, and objective pressure banners over the campaign map
fidelityFocus.logisticsReadiness: descriptor-driven supply depots, forage corridors, road strain, courier order tempo, siege readiness, and winter attrition warnings over the campaign map
fidelityFocus.diplomaticCommandReadiness: descriptor-driven senate mandates, tribute obligations, ally loyalty, rebellion sparks, province pacification, and triumph windows over the campaign map
fidelityFocus.regions: oversized highlighted region meshes with glow, wobble borders, and DSK affordance descriptors
fidelityFocus.visibleUi: minimal route chrome; reusable command/readability logic remains descriptor-owned
fidelityFocus.uiDom: presentation-only sinks and command surfaces; campaign truth stays in the campaign state and domain descriptors

proceduralVegetation.grass: deterministic grass descriptor field is retained as metadata for future terrain-anchored instancing
proceduralVegetation.screenSpaceRendering: disabled because it was visually floating in the sky and caused panning/selection jank
proceduralVegetation.battlefieldGrounding: tile features draw directly on the hex playfield so they cannot detach from terrain
proceduralVegetation.boundary: local procedural vegetation descriptor DSK candidate owns placements; no screen-space overlay is rendered until true terrain-anchored WebGPU instancing exists

fidelityFocus.soldiers: tactical hex mode uses mini low-poly soldier squads with individual angled shadows, body class colors, military bands, shields, spears, helmets, and selection pennants
fidelityFocus.battlefield: aligned WebGL2-shaded hex grid, terrain tiles, scene-native highlights, shaded dice, aggregated squad pieces, region terrain variation, and no circular token rings

protectedExpressionGuardrails: Do not copy tabletop scenario layouts, card systems, dice faces, command sections, rule text, or unit-stat tables.
Keep the route visual-first until the cinematic terrain and battlefield proof is stable.
Use Roman-history flavor as original visual composition, not as a digital recreation of a board-game ruleset.
currentLocalSurfaces: realistic terrain mesh color and brush-stroke overlay generation
large pannable region ellipse placement and labels
procedural vegetation descriptor generation without screen-space presentation
Rome-perspective aligned WebGL2 hex battlefield overlay
campaign map cell ownership and one-action cadence

rendererOwnership.hexGameplayOverlay: scene-native movement/highlight/squad renderer and first playable interaction layer
rendererOwnership.campaignFractalOverlay: presentation-only Canvas2D consumer of renderer-neutral descriptors
rendererOwnership.logisticsReadinessOverlay: presentation-only Canvas2D consumer of renderer-neutral logistics descriptors
rendererOwnership.diplomaticCommandOverlay: presentation-only Canvas2D consumer of renderer-neutral diplomatic command descriptors
dskOwned: procedural vegetation descriptor snapshot via local DSK candidate
hex battlefield snapshot via local DSK candidate
tactical gameplay snapshot via local DSK candidate
campaign fractal descriptor snapshot via local DSK candidate
battlefield orders descriptor snapshot via local DSK candidate
logistics readiness descriptor snapshot via local DSK candidate
diplomatic command descriptor snapshot via local DSK candidate

futureProtoKitCandidatesFromCustomLogic.reason: Reusable frontier pressure and supply readability for turn-based strategy maps.
Reusable adjacent-move and attack outcome preview descriptors.
Reusable formation density and troop power readability.
Reusable ownership pressure and front-line weather descriptors.
Reusable command availability descriptors independent of DOM buttons.
Reusable scouting and next-neighbor intent descriptors for strategy maps.
Reusable exposed-edge risk descriptors for territory and formation games.
Reusable attack/defense outcome readability independent of the renderer.
Reusable supply, forage, road strain, courier, siege, and winter-readiness descriptors for strategy maps.
Reusable senate mandate, ally loyalty, rebellion, pacification, and triumph-window descriptors for strategy maps.

intentionallyOmitted: unit stat tables beyond visual class and terrain metadata
campaign economy beyond descriptor-only tribute pressure
nextLedge: Add a deterministic campaign replay that chooses a Roman cell, previews scout vectors, checks senate pressure, commits one march or envoy move, ends a world turn, and snapshot-hashes campaign, logistics, diplomatic command, and battlefield-orders renderer handoff descriptors.
